strengths: 18+ years of full-stack experience with deep, recent TypeScript/Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS work (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq) — directly matching the primary stack and exceeding the 6-year bar
Hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — a rare, explicitly required skill this candidate demonstrably has

Exceptional testing and engineering-craftsmanship credentials: TDD/pairing/trunk-based practice, author of test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and creator of multi-day testing courses — ideal for code-review quality and clean, well-tested code
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience listed, though extensive Node.js backend work suggests a short ramp-up
Kotlin is absent and Java experience is dated (robotics work 2016–2018), so the secondary JVM stack would need refreshing
Career gap since October 2025, though it follows nearly two decades of continuous employment

key_factors.factor: AI agent development experience (MCP, agentic tooling)

response.justification: The candidate is a strong match for this senior fullstack role: he brings well over the required six years of end-to-end product experience centered on the exact primary stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, Docker/Kubernetes), and — unusually — direct professional experience building AI-agent infrastructure with MCP, satisfying the hardest-to-find requirement. His track record spans architecture-level contributions (high-throughput microservices, event-driven platforms), customer-facing product delivery, and DevOps, while his TDD practice, authored testing tools, and teaching background align squarely with the role's emphasis on code quality, reviews, and process improvement. Gaps around Nest.js and Kotlin are framework-level rather than conceptual, and his demonstrated polyglot range (TypeScript, Java, C++, D, Swift, PHP) suggests he would close them quickly.
